title: Lively Zhejiang Tofu Stir-Fry
description: A crisp, charred tofu dish simmered in a sweet sugar syrup with savory wok-hei, delivering a zesty, energizing flavor perfect for lunch.
instructions: Press tofu between paper towels and cube into 1-inch pieces.
Heat wok over high heat and add sesame oil until shimmering.
Add tofu cubes and stir-fry until golden brown on all sides, about 5 minutes, then remove to plate.
In same wok, sauté minced garlic, grated ginger, and scallion for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Stir in sugar, soy sauce, black vinegar, and water; bring to a gentle boil.
Whisk cornstarch with a splash of water to make a slurry, then pour into wok and stir until the sauce thickens.
Return tofu to wok, toss to coat with glossy sauce, and sprinkle with sesame seeds and chili flakes.
Cook for an additional 2 minutes, then transfer to serving dish and serve hot.
